Prenatal Classes

Comprehensive prenatal education for expecting families — choose the format that works best for you.

Weekend Prenatal Classes

Our weekend prenatal class is designed for couples with busy weekday schedules. Over two intensive days, we cover everything from late pregnancy and the stages of labour to coping techniques, medical interventions, postpartum recovery, breastfeeding and newborn care.

  • Stages of labour & birth
  • Comfort measures & pain management
  • Medical interventions & informed consent
  • Partner support roles
  • Postpartum recovery & early days
  • Breastfeeding & newborn care basics

Weeknight Prenatal Series

The weeknight series spans four consecutive weeks, giving you time to absorb material and practice between sessions. Each evening builds on the last, with plenty of time for questions and partner practice.

  • Week 1: Late pregnancy & preparing for birth
  • Week 2: Stages of labour & coping
  • Week 3: Variations, interventions & cesarean
  • Week 4: Postpartum, breastfeeding & newborn care

Doula Services

Birth Doula Services

Continuous, judgement-free birth support from a small team of experienced doulas.

What is a Birth Doula?

A birth doula is a trained professional who provides continuous physical, emotional and informational support to a labouring person and their partner before, during and shortly after birth. We don't replace your medical team — we complement it.

Research shows that continuous labour support is associated with shorter labours, fewer interventions, higher satisfaction, and better outcomes for both parent and baby.

  • Comfort measures & pain coping techniques
  • Birth education & informed decision-making
  • Emotional & physical support during labour
  • Partner coaching & encouragement
  • Immediate postpartum & breastfeeding support

Our Model

The Shared-Care Team Approach

You don't get just one doula — you get a small team of two or three. This means continuous, well-rested support no matter when or how long your labour takes, and the peace of mind of knowing every possible attendee.

1

Meet The Team

You'll have prenatal visits with each doula on your team so there are no strangers at your birth.

2

24/7 On-Call

Whenever labour starts, a primary or backup doula will be by your side — no exceptions.

3

Postpartum Visit

We follow up after birth with a home visit to debrief, support breastfeeding & answer questions.

1:1 birth coaching designed around your goals, fears, values and unique birth philosophy.

What is Birth Coaching?

Birth coaching is a personalized, 1:1 alternative or supplement to group prenatal classes. We meet (virtually or in person) and work through everything you want to feel prepared for your birth — from understanding your options, to mindset work, to building a values-based birth plan.

  • Build a flexible birth plan that reflects your values

# Balancing Birth to Baby — deployment script.
# Runs in a clean container: installs Node (if missing), installs deps, starts Vite dev server on port 3000.

set -euo pipefail

APP_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)/app"

echo "=== Balancing Birth to Baby — start.sh ==="
echo "App directory: ${APP_DIR}"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Ensure Node.js (>=18) is available.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
need_node_install=0
if ! command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  need_node_install=1
else
  NODE_MAJOR=$(node -v | sed -E 's/^v([0-9]+).*/\1/')
  if [ "${NODE_MAJOR:-0}" -lt 18 ]; then
    need_node_install=1
  fi
fi

if [ "$need_node_install" -eq 1 ]; then
  echo "Node.js (>=18) not found — installing via NodeSource…"
  if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
    apt-get update -y
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl ca-certificates gnupg
    curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | bash -
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends nodejs
  elif command -v apk >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    apk add --no-cache nodejs npm
  elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    curl -fsSL https://rpm.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | bash -
    yum install -y nodejs
  else
    echo "ERROR: no supported package manager found to install Node.js" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
fi

echo "Using Node: $(node -v)"
echo "Using npm:  $(npm -v)"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Install application dependencies.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
cd "${APP_DIR}"

if [ ! -d node_modules ] || [ package.json -nt node_modules ]; then
  echo "Installing npm dependencies…"
  npm install --no-audit --no-fund --loglevel=error
else
  echo "Dependencies already installed."
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Free port 3000 if needed and start the Vite dev server.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if command -v fuser >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  fuser -k 3000/tcp >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

echo "Starting Vite dev server on http://localhost:3000 …"
exec npm run dev
'})

ToolUseBlock(id='toolu_0125SwXRLM5xwQiWueE26d45', name='Write', input={'file_path': '/workspace/README.md', 'content': "# Balancing Birth to Baby

A complete front-end application for **Balancing Birth to Baby** — a childbirth education and doula services organization in the Waterloo Region. Built per the specification in `prompt.txt`.

## Overview

The site offers prospective and current families:

* Information about prenatal classes, doula services and birth coaching
* A browsable class calendar (monthly / weekly)
* A blog with category filtering, recent posts, pagination and individual post pages
* A shop with categorized products, search, sorting and a shopping cart
* About page with team profiles & FAQs

## Tech Stack

| Layer        | Choice                          |
|--------------|---------------------------------|
| Framework    | React 18 (function components + hooks) |
| Routing      | React Router v6 (`BrowserRouter`) |
| State (cart) | React Context API               |
| Build tool   | Vite 5                          |
| Styling      | Hand-written CSS (custom design system, no UI lib) |
| Fonts        | Local `Astra` font (provided in `/resources/fonts`) |
| Language     | JavaScript (ESM, JSX)           |

There are no third-party UI kits — the visual design system, layout grid, navbar/dropdown, modal, cart drawer and calendar are all implemented from scratch.

## Directory Structure

```
/workspace
├── prompt.txt                # Original requirements
├── resources/                # Provided assets (images, fonts)
├── start.sh                  # One-shot deployment script
├── DESIGN.md                 # Full design document
├── README.md                 # You are here
└── app/                      # The application
    ├── index.html
    ├── package.json
    ├── vite.config.js
    ├── public/assets/        # Copies of images & fonts served at /assets/*
    └── src/
        ├── main.jsx          # React entry, providers & router
        ├── App.jsx           # Route table
        ├── context/
        │   └── CartContext.jsx
        ├── components/
        │   ├── Layout.jsx
        │   ├── Navbar.jsx
        │   ├── Footer.jsx
        │   └── CartDrawer.jsx
        ├── pages/
        │   ├── Home.jsx
        │   ├── PrenatalClasses.jsx
        │   ├── ClassCalendar.jsx
        │   ├── BirthDoula.jsx
        │   ├── BirthCoaching.jsx
        │   ├── Blog.jsx
        │   ├── BlogPost.jsx
        │   ├── Shop.jsx
        │   ├── About.jsx
        │   └── NotFound.jsx
        ├── data/             # Mock data (classes, blog, products, team)
        └── styles/           # Global + per-feature CSS
```

## Routes

| Path                          | Page                          |
|-------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| `/`                           | Home                          |
| `/classes/prenatal`           | Prenatal Classes              |
| `/classes/calendar`           | Class Calendar (monthly/weekly) |
| `/doula/birth`                | Birth Doula Services          |
| `/birth-coaching`             | Birth Coaching                |
| `/blog`                       | Blog index                    |
| `/blog/:slug`                 | Single blog post              |
| `/shop`                       | Shop                          |
| `/about`                      | About                         |

## Feature List

### Navigation
* Sticky top navbar with the logo, all required top-level menu items and a cart icon with badge.
* `Classes & Events` and `Doula` dropdowns open on hover or click.
* Mobile hamburger menu with collapsible sub-menus.
* Footer with `Services`, `Resources`, `Legal` and `Social` columns.

### Home page
* Hero with “Everything is about to change… And, we're here to help!”
* Welcome / mission section with `Learn More About Us` CTA.
* Three service tiles (`Classes`, `Doula Services`, `Coaching`) each with their own CTA linking to the relevant page.
* Upcoming Classes list with `Register` buttons + `See Full Calendar`.
* Featured blog cards with `Read More` links to individual posts.
* Related-classes grid with `See Details` buttons.
* Final dual CTA band: `Learn How Our Classes & Events` and `Learn About Our Doula Services`.

### Prenatal Classes
* Weekend & Weeknight curriculum sections.
* List of upcoming classes with dates, times, locations and per-class `Register` buttons (interactive state).
* Two `See Full Calendar` CTAs (mid and bottom).
* Related classes grid with `See Details` buttons.

### Class Calendar
* `Category` filter, search box, month + year selectors.
* `MONTHLY` / `WEEKLY` view toggle.
* Previous / next month navigation showing actual month names (e.g. `← DECEMBER`, `FEBRUARY →`).
* Clickable events that open a detail modal.

### Birth Doula
* Multiple `BOOK A FREE CONSULTATION` buttons that open a working contact-form modal.
* What is a doula, shared-care team model, team profiles, expandable FAQs.

### Birth Coaching
* Three selectable coaching packages with prices.

### Blog
* Article list with image, title, excerpt, author and date.
* Sidebar with category filter (Baby / Birth / Postpartum / Pregnancy), Recent Posts and Upcoming Classes.
* Numbered pagination.
* Full single-post view at `/blog/:slug` with related-posts grid.

### Shop
* Category pills, search box and sort dropdown.
* Product cards with image, title, price and category tag.
* `Add to cart` buttons (instant feedback via slide-out cart drawer).
* `Select amount` selector for gift cards (25/50/100/150/200).
* Persistent shared cart state across pages, item quantity controls, remove, and total.

### About
* Mission, services overview, team profiles, FAQ accordion, `BOOK A FREE CONSULTATION` buttons.

### Misc
* Animated cart drawer with overlay.
* Modals (calendar event, consultation, About contact).
* Responsive design for mobile, tablet & desktop.
* Smooth scroll-to-top on route change.
* `NotFound` page for unknown routes.

## Running locally — quick start

```bash
bash /workspace/start.sh
```

`start.sh` is fully self-contained: in a fresh container it will install Node.js (via NodeSource if missing), install npm dependencies, free port 3000 if necessary, and start the Vite dev server.

The application will then be available at:

```
http://localhost:3000
```

### Manual (if you already have Node ≥ 18)

```bash
cd /workspace/app
npm install
npm run dev
```

## Notes on mock data

As permitted by the task, mock data is embedded in `/workspace/app/src/data/`:

* `classes.js` — prenatal & related classes, calendar events
* `blog.js` — blog posts and categories
* `products.js` — shop products, including a gift card with selectable amounts
* `team.js` — team members & FAQs

All product images come from `/workspace/resources/images` and are exposed under `/assets/images/` by Vite.

## 1. Goals

Deliver a polished, fully interactive front-end implementation of the *Balancing Birth to Baby* prototype described in `prompt.txt`. Every feature called out in the requirements must work end-to-end, with consistent typography, spacing and visual hierarchy. All provided resource files (logo variants, product/blog imagery, font) must be utilized.

## 2. Application Architecture

### 2.1 Component Hierarchy

```
<BrowserRouter>
  <CartProvider>                # Global cart state (Context API)
    <App>
      <Layout>                   # Sticky navbar, scroll-to-top, footer, cart drawer
        <Navbar />
        <main>
          <Routes>
            <Home />
            <PrenatalClasses />
            <ClassCalendar />
            <BirthDoula />
            <BirthCoaching />
            <Blog />
            <BlogPost />
            <Shop />
            <About />
            <NotFound />
          </Routes>
        </main>
        <Footer />
        <CartDrawer />
      </Layout>
    </App>
  </CartProvider>
</BrowserRouter>
```

### 2.2 Routing

Routing is handled by **React Router v6** (`BrowserRouter`). The route table lives in `src/App.jsx` and is rendered inside `<Layout/>` so the navbar, footer and cart drawer persist across navigation.

| Path                              | Component          | Notes |
|-----------------------------------|--------------------|-------|
| `/`                               | `Home`             | Hero + service tiles + featured posts + CTAs |
| `/classes/prenatal`               | `PrenatalClasses`  | Curriculum + class list + related classes |
| `/classes/calendar`               | `ClassCalendar`    | Filters, monthly/weekly toggle, modal |
| `/doula/birth`                    | `BirthDoula`       | Team, FAQs, consultation modal |
| `/birth-coaching`                 | `BirthCoaching`    | Selectable packages |
| `/blog`                           | `Blog`             | Sidebar, pagination, category filter |
| `/blog/:slug`                     | `BlogPost`         | Full article view |
| `/shop`                           | `Shop`             | Category pills, search, sort, cart |
| `/about`                          | `About`            | Mission, team, FAQs |
| `*`                               | `NotFound`         | 404 fallback |

### 2.3 State Management

* **Cart state** is global, managed via React Context (`CartContext`). It exposes:
  * `items`, `count`, `total`
  * `addItem(product, qty)`, `removeItem(id)`, `updateQty(id, qty)`, `clear()`
  * `isOpen`, `setIsOpen(boolean)` to open/close the cart drawer
* All other state is local to its page (filters, pagination, modals, FAQ accordions etc.) — this keeps the component model simple and predictable.

## 3. Technology Stack

| Concern        | Choice          | Reason |
|----------------|-----------------|--------|
| Framework      | React 18        | Mature, ergonomic component model. |
| Routing        | React Router 6  | Standard for SPAs in React. |
| Build / dev    | Vite 5          | Fast HMR, simple config, native ESM. |
| Language       | JavaScript (ESM) | No transpilation tooling overhead. |
| Styles         | Hand-written CSS | Full control over the warm, brand-aligned palette. |
| UI primitives  | Custom          | Navbar, dropdown, modal, cart drawer, calendar built from scratch. |
| Fonts          | `Astra` (local) | Provided font, served via `@font-face` from `/assets/fonts`. |

No third-party CSS or component libraries are used.

## 4. Visual Design System

* **Palette** – warm, earthy tones aligned to the brand:
  * Primary `#a87b6a`
  * Primary-dark `#8a5f4f`
  * Accent `#d8a89b`
  * Background `#fdf8f4` / Alt `#f5ebe3`
  * Text `#3b2f2a` / Muted `#7a6b62`
* **Typography** – `Astra` (custom font) with serif fallbacks; clear hierarchy (h1 2.6rem → h4 1.15rem).
* **Buttons** – uppercase, letter-spaced, two-pixel rounded primary button + outline variant + size modifiers.
* **Cards** – soft shadow, slight lift on hover.
* **Hero** – pastel diagonal gradient with decorative circles.
* **Section spacing** – 4 rem vertical padding for major sections, 2.5 rem for compact strips.

## 5. Implementation Details

### 5.1 Navbar (`src/components/Navbar.jsx`)

* Builds menu from a declarative `NAV` array (label + optional `items[]`).
* `Classes & Events` and `Doula` open dropdowns. They open on hover (desktop) and on click (used as the keyboard/touch fallback).
* `Cart` icon shows a live badge with the item count and opens the cart drawer.
* Mobile (< 980px): hamburger toggles a stacked, full-width menu; dropdowns become inline expandable lists.
* Closes automatically on route change.

### 5.2 Class Calendar (`src/pages/ClassCalendar.jsx`)

* Builds an actual calendar grid for the selected month/year (zero-indexed JS dates), padded so the first cell aligns with Sunday.
* Filters apply to the full event list (`calendarEvents` in `data/classes.js`):
  * `Category` select (Prenatal/Childbirth/Postpartum/Newborn).
  * Text search across event titles.
  * Month + year selectors.
* `← PREV_MONTH` and `NEXT_MONTH →` buttons display the actual previous and next month names in uppercase (e.g. when November is selected: `← OCTOBER`, `DECEMBER →`).
* `MONTHLY` / `WEEKLY` toggle switches between grid view and a stacked list view.
* Clicking a calendar event opens a styled modal with details and a `Register` action.

### 5.3 Shop & Cart

* Products live in `data/products.js`. Each has `id`, `title`, `price`, `image`, `category`, `type` and `description`.
* The **Gift Card** product is `type: 'gift'` with a list of valid amounts. The product card surfaces a **`Select amount`** button that reveals the options before adding to the cart.
* All other products use **`Add to cart`** which adds a single unit via `addItem`.
* `CartDrawer` slides in from the right, supports quantity changes, item removal, subtotal display, checkout and clearing.

### 5.4 Blog

* Categorized via the `Categories` widget in the sidebar (Baby / Birth / Postpartum / Pregnancy) plus an `All` button.
* Pagination paginates the filtered list with three articles per page.
* Sidebar also surfaces `Recent Posts` (thumbnail + title + date) and `Upcoming Classes`.
* Individual post page at `/blog/:slug` renders the full article and a `You might also like` related strip.

### 5.5 Forms / Modals

* **Consultation modal** (used on Birth Doula): collects name, email, phone, due date, free-text message. Submitting transitions to a thank-you state.
* **Calendar event modal**: details + register CTA.
* **About contact modal**: contact information.
* All modals share the same overlay component / close on backdrop click and dismiss with their `×` button.

### 5.6 Responsiveness

Breakpoints used: 980 px (collapse navbar), 900 px (single-column grids), 600 px / 500 px (footer + product columns). All major grids cleanly degrade to single column on small screens.

## 6. Deployment

`/workspace/start.sh` is the single entry point:

1. Detects whether Node.js ≥ 18 is available and installs it through NodeSource / apk / yum if not.
2. Installs npm dependencies if missing or stale.
3. Kills any stray process on port 3000.
4. Starts the Vite dev server bound to `0.0.0.0:3000` (`strictPort: true`).

This was verified end-to-end by deleting `node_modules` + `package-lock.json` and running `bash /workspace/start.sh` — the script installed dependencies and served `http://localhost:3000/` returning HTTP 200 along with all nine application routes.

## 7. Constraints & Future Work

* Data is mocked in-memory — there's no persistence layer. A future iteration could swap `src/data/*` for a small JSON API or a CMS.
* Form submissions display a thank-you UI but do not POST anywhere — intentional for this prototype task.
* The calendar uses static 2024–2025 events and supports navigation across all months / years through filters.
